channel.title: NPR Topics: Space
channel.description: NPR coverage of space exploration, space shuttle missions, news from NASA, private space exploration, satellite technology, and new discoveries in astronomy and astrophysics.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Satellite images provide view inside Iran at war
item.description: Satellite images from commercial companies show the extent of U.S. and Israeli strikes, and how Iran is responding.
item.content:encoded: Satellite images from commercial companies show the extent of U.S. and Israeli strikes, and how Iran is responding.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: NASA redirects Artemis moon mission program, postponing a planned astronaut landing
item.description: In shaking up its Artemis lunar program, NASA's new moon plan looks more like the Apollo missions of the 1960s. Instead of landing on the surface on Artemis III, NASA hopes to do so on Artemis IV.
item.content:encoded:
In shaking up its Artemis lunar program, NASA's new moon plan looks more like the Apollo missions of the 1960s. Instead of landing on the surface on Artemis III, NASA hopes to do so on Artemis IV.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: NASA lost a lunar spacecraft one day after launch. A new report details what went wrong
item.description: Why did a $72 million mission to study water on the moon fail so soon after launch? A new NASA report has the answer.
item.content:encoded:
Why did a $72 million mission to study water on the moon fail so soon after launch? A new NASA report has the answer.

(Image credit: Lockheed Martin)
item.dc:creator: Joe Palca

item.title: NASA's Artemis II lunar mission may not launch in March after all
item.description: NASA says an "interrupted flow" of helium to the rocket system could require a rollback to the Vehicle Assembly Building. If it happens, NASA says the launch to the moon would be delayed until April.
item.content:encoded:
NASA says an "interrupted flow" of helium to the rocket system could require a rollback to the Vehicle Assembly Building. If it happens, NASA says the launch to the moon would be delayed until April.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: NASA eyes March 6 to launch 4 astronauts to the moon on Artemis II mission
item.description: The four astronauts heading to the moon for the lunar fly-by are the first humans to venture there since 1972. The ten-day mission will travel more than 600,000 miles.
item.content:encoded:
The four astronauts heading to the moon for the lunar fly-by are the first humans to venture there since 1972. The ten-day mission will travel more than 600,000 miles.

(Image credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: NASA chief blasts Boeing, space agency for failed Starliner astronaut mission
item.description: NASA's Jared Isaacman slammed Boeing for failures with its Starliner spacecraft, which was deemed unsafe to return its crew of two astronauts from the International Space Station
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Jared Isaacman slammed Boeing for failures with its Starliner spacecraft, which was deemed unsafe to return its crew of two astronauts from the International Space Station

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Four people on NASA's Crew-12 arrive at the International Space Station
item.description: The crew will spend the next eight months conducting experiments to prepare for human exploration beyond Earth's orbit.
item.content:encoded:
The crew will spend the next eight months conducting experiments to prepare for human exploration beyond Earth's orbit.

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Lydia Calitri

item.title: On their way! 4 people on NASA Crew-12 mission launch to International Space Station
item.description: The four are set to dock with the space station on Saturday, returning the orbital lab to its full complement of seven. NASA's last mission, Crew-11, left a month early due to an ill crew member.
item.content:encoded:
The four are set to dock with the space station on Saturday, returning the orbital lab to its full complement of seven. NASA's last mission, Crew-11, left a month early due to an ill crew member.

(Image credit: SpaceX via NASA)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: NASA delays the launch of Artemis II lunar mission by at least a month
item.description: NASA is targeting March for the launch of four astronauts on a ten-day mission to circle the moon and return safely to Earth, traveling farther than any humans have ventured in deep space.
item.content:encoded:
NASA is targeting March for the launch of four astronauts on a ten-day mission to circle the moon and return safely to Earth, traveling farther than any humans have ventured in deep space.

(Image credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: What to know about Artemis II's 'wet dress rehearsal'
item.description: As astronauts prepare to fly around the moon, critical testing must occur before there is "Go" for launch.
item.content:encoded:
As astronauts prepare to fly around the moon, critical testing must occur before there is "Go" for launch.

(Image credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster
